input.background: The average annual salary of 20 female hospital administrators is 80,900. These administrators believe they make less than their male counterparts because of sex bias. Published results suggest that hospital administrators have an average salary of μ= 85,100(σ=10,000).
input.question: Sketch the sampling distribution of x̄ based on an SRS of n=20. Assume μ=85,100 and σ= 10,000. Mark the horizontal axis with landmarks that are ±1 and ±2 standard errors around μ. Locate x̄= 80,900 on the horizontal axis of the curve.

output.answer: First note that SE_x̄=10000/√20=2236. Under the null hypothesis x̄ ∼ N(85100, 2236). The sketch of x̄ ∼ N(85100,2236) is not shown in this key. The sampling distribution of x̄ should be Normal with landmarks on the curve starting 2 standard errors below the mean at 80,628,82,864,85,100,87,336, and 89,572.

sample_mean.description: The sample mean salary of 20 female hospital administrators.

population_mean.description: The population mean salary of hospital administrators.

population_standard_deviation.description: The population standard deviation of hospital administrators' salaries.

sample_size.description: The size of the sample of female hospital administrators.
